method: uniform random sample (random.Random(seed).sample), every sampled detection read and judged by a human from the raw evidence; FP buckets only describe/aggregate the confirmed FPs
active_verification: NONE -- the finding schema has no verified/Verified field; TruffleHog ran unverified, so no live-verification signal exists. Verdicts are manual ground truth.

tp_examples_redacted.reason: SSH host private key in /etc/ssh -- a real host key shipped in the image (same TP class as the main paper).
AWS/Okta-style access key (value ends in 0oa...) captured in an application provisioning log -- real credential leaked to log.
Google API key (AIza... prefix) baked into a production frontend JS bundle (app-owned, not test/dependency).
PEM private key embedded in an application config source file gcpSecretKey.js (non-test app path).
Second AWS/Okta-style access key captured in the same application provisioning log.

candidate_examples_redacted.reason: TLS client private key .pem at the image app root (/Viperviz). App-owned key file, but could be a shipped sample -- not confirmed live; conservatively FP.

comparison_main_paper.agreement: baseline FP rate is within / consistent with the main paper's 99.7% (overlapping Wilson intervals); same dominant FP classes (package hashes, library bytes, example URIs)
